title: Modern Operators
content:
### Modern JavaScript Operators

ES2020+ introduced powerful operators for **safer, cleaner code**.

---

### Optional Chaining (`?.`)

Access nested properties **without checking each level**:

```js
const user = { profile: { name: "John" } };

// Old way (error-prone)
const name = user && user.profile && user.profile.name;

// With optional chaining
const name = user?.profile?.name; // "John"
const city = user?.address?.city; // undefined (no error!)
```

Also works with **method calls** and **dynamic access**:

```js
const result = obj.method?.();      // Only calls if method exists
const value = arr?.[0];             // Works with arrays
```

---

### Nullish Coalescing (`??`)

Returns the **right-side** value only if the left side is `null` or `undefined`:

```js
const name = input ?? "default";
```

| Left Value | Result |
|------------|--------|
| `"John"` | `"John"` |
| `null` | `"default"` |
| `undefined` | `"default"` |
| `0` | `0` (not replaced!) |
| `""` | `""` (not replaced!) |

> **Key Difference:** `??` only replaces `null`/`undefined`. The `||` operator replaces **all falsy** values (including `0`, `""`, `false`).

---

### Logical Assignment Operators

| Operator | Equivalent To | Example |
|----------|--------------|---------|
| `&&=` | `x = x && y` | `x &&= 5` |
| `||=` | `x = x || y` | `x ||= 5` |
| `??=` | `x = x ?? y` | `x ??= 5` |

```js
let a = true;
a &&= false;    // a = false (only assigns if a is truthy)

let b = null;
b ??= "default"; // b = "default" (only assigns if b is null/undefined)

let c = 0;
c ||= 10;       // c = 10 (|| replaces all falsy)
c ??= 10;       // c = 0 (?? does NOT replace 0)
```

---

> **These operators** make code more concise and prevent common bugs with nested property access and default values.
